name: Cincinnati 5-way Chili
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the butter in a large heavy skillet over medium-high heat.
2. Add the ground beef to the skillet and break up any lumps with a fork. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the meat is evenly browned.
3. Stir in all of the remaining ingredients through water.
4. Taste and adjust seasonings. If the flavor is too sweet, add a small amount of vinegar; if not spicy enough, add a small amount of ground chile.
5. Bring the mixture to a boil, then lower the heat and simmer, uncovered, for 2 to 4 hours.
6. Add the kidney beans to the mixture about 1/2 hour before serving.
7. Serve in individual bowls with a small amount of cooked vermicelli.
8. Spoon chili onto each portion and top with grated cheese and raw onion or pass the cheese and onions on the side.
